questions.question_text: Under AREC Regulation 10.5, which name must appear in all real estate advertising?
questions.correct_answer: The firm name as recorded with AREC
distractors: The agent's personal legal name
The team name or DBA
The principal broker's home address
questions.explanation: The firm name as recorded with AREC must appear in all advertising. It must be in equal to or greater prominence than any licensee name, team name, or DBA.

questions.question_text: An agent's online ad shows the team name in 24-point font and the firm name in 10-point font. This:
distractors: Is acceptable for digital advertising
Is fine as long as both names appear
Only matters for print advertising
questions.explanation: The firm name must be in equal to or greater prominence than any licensee or team name. A 10-point firm name with a 24-point team name violates this rule.

questions.question_text: What penalty does an agent face for a non-compliant advertisement in Arkansas?
distractors: Immediate license suspension
A $1,000 fine
A formal hearing before AREC
questions.explanation: Non-compliant advertisements result in a $100 citation to the agent. Repeated violations could lead to more serious disciplinary action.

questions.question_text: What contact information must be included in all advertising?
questions.correct_answer: Information enabling contact with the Principal Broker
distractors: The agent's personal cell phone number
The AREC complaint hotline number
The property's physical street address
questions.explanation: Advertising must include firm contact information that enables communication with the Principal Broker. This ensures consumers can reach the responsible broker.

questions.question_text: Who must develop and maintain an advertising compliance policy for the firm?
distractors: Each individual agent
The Executive Broker only
AREC's compliance division
questions.explanation: The Principal Broker must develop and maintain an advertising compliance policy for the firm. This is part of the broker's overall supervision responsibilities.

questions.question_text: A licensee wants to purchase a listed property for personal use. What must they do?
questions.correct_answer: Provide full disclosure of their interest
distractors: Resign their license before purchasing
Obtain AREC approval for the purchase
Have another agent handle the entire deal
questions.explanation: When a licensee has a personal interest in a transaction, full disclosure is required. Self-dealing without disclosure is a violation of the license law.

questions.question_text: A salesperson buys an investment property listed by their own firm without disclosing their license status. This is:
distractors: Acceptable since they used a different agent
Only a problem if they got a reduced price
Permitted for investment properties
questions.explanation: Self-dealing without disclosure violates the license law regardless of whether the licensee uses a different agent within the firm. The personal interest must be disclosed.

questions.question_text: Self-dealing disclosure applies to which types of licensee interests?
questions.correct_answer: Purchases, sales, and any beneficial interest
distractors: Only direct purchases by the licensee
Only transactions over $100,000
Only when the licensee is the listing agent
questions.explanation: Self-dealing disclosure applies broadly — to purchases, sales, or any beneficial interest the licensee has in a transaction, including indirect ownership through entities.

questions.question_text: A broker lists a property owned by their spouse. What disclosure is required?
questions.correct_answer: The broker's personal interest must be disclosed
distractors: No disclosure needed for spousal property
Only disclosure to AREC, not the buyer
Disclosure only at the closing table
questions.explanation: A spouse's ownership represents a beneficial interest. The broker must disclose this personal connection to all parties in the transaction.

questions.question_text: Failure to disclose self-dealing in Arkansas can result in:
distractors: Only a verbal warning
A $100 advertising citation
No penalty if the transaction was fair
questions.explanation: Self-dealing without disclosure is a violation of the license law and can result in AREC disciplinary action including suspension, revocation, or monetary penalties.

questions.question_text: An agent refers clients to a specific home inspector and receives a referral fee. What is required?
questions.correct_answer: The referral fee arrangement must be disclosed
distractors: Referral fees are always prohibited
No disclosure is needed for inspectors
Only the buyer needs to be informed
questions.explanation: Third-party service provider relationships and compensation arrangements must be transparent. Agents must disclose any referral fees or kickbacks to all relevant parties.

questions.question_text: Compensation arrangements between agents and service providers must be:
distractors: Kept confidential as business matters
Reported to AREC annually
Limited to 10% of the service cost
questions.explanation: Arkansas law requires transparency in compensation arrangements with third-party service providers. Hidden fees or undisclosed referral arrangements violate the license law.

questions.question_text: A listing agent steers all clients to a title company owned by the agent's family. No disclosure is made. This is:
questions.correct_answer: A violation requiring disclosure of the relationship
distractors: Acceptable business practice
Only a violation if the title company charges more
Permitted if the client doesn't ask
questions.explanation: Third-party service provider relationships must be disclosed. Steering clients to a family-owned company without disclosure violates the transparency requirement.

questions.question_text: Which of the following BEST describes Arkansas requirements for agent-service provider relationships?
questions.correct_answer: All financial relationships must be disclosed
distractors: Agents cannot receive any referral compensation
Only written referral agreements are regulated
Disclosure is voluntary for service providers
questions.explanation: Arkansas requires disclosure of all financial relationships between agents and third-party service providers. This includes referral fees, ownership interests, and compensation arrangements.

questions.question_text: An agent receives a gift card from a lender for referring clients. Under Arkansas law, the agent must:
questions.correct_answer: Disclose the arrangement to referred clients
distractors: Return the gift card to the lender
Report the gift to the IRS only
Accept it only if under $25 in value
questions.explanation: Any compensation — including gifts — from third-party service providers must be disclosed to clients. Transparency in all referral and compensation arrangements is required.
